title: Graduate Trainee Underwriter
description: **Job Title:** Graduate Trainee Underwriter – Surety **Reporting to:** Head of Surety \- Ireland **Location:** Kill, Co. Kildare. Ireland **Position Type:** Full time. Permanent. Initially during the training process, it is 5 days a week in the office but after successful completion of training it is 3 days in the office and 2 working from home. Overview: **Why** **Tokio Marine HCC?** Standing still is not an option in the current world of Insurance. TMHCC are one of the world’s leading Specialty Insurers. With deep expertise in our chosen lines of business, our unparalleled track record and a solid balance sheet, TMHCC evaluates and manages risk like no one else in the industry. Looking beyond profit, empowering our people and delivering on our commitments are at the core of our customer values, and so is a desire to grow and provide creative and innovative solutions to our clients. Are you a motivated and ambitious graduate in Law, Finance, or Business looking to apply your skills in a commercial, global environment—without following the traditional practice route? Do you have the ability to analyse financial information and understand how financial statements tell the story of a business? If so, this is a fantastic opportunity to build a rewarding career with strong earning potential, outside of a traditional legal or financial practice, with full study support provided. **Job Purpose:** We are looking for a motivated and analytical graduate to join our European Surety Underwriting Team within Tokio Marine HCC (TMHCC). This role offers a unique opportunity to develop technical and commercial expertise in a niche area of insurance, working alongside experienced underwriters across a wide European network. The Surety division at TMHCC provides bonds and guarantees relating to contractual and regulatory obligations for clients based globally, with a primary focus on Western Europe. You will work as part of a collaborative, international team with offices across: London, Leicester, Paris, Amsterdam, Copenhagen, Madrid, Milan \& Frankfurt. As a result, some travel will be required as part of this role. This role offers a clear and structured career pathway, with the opportunity to progress into an Underwriting position within approximately 3–5 years, depending on performance. This will be done by giving you exposure to complex, international cases and clients. There is a strong long\-term earnings potential as you advance within the business **Key Responsibilities:** * Underwriting of surety risks in line with agreed strategy and personal authority limits. * Review and agreement of legal documents (such as bond forms, security documents, contracts and other agreements). * Building relationships with internal and external stakeholders and clients. Liaising effectively with clients and brokers. * Gather relevant and sufficient information both financial and other, to assess financial strength of existing and potential clients. * Researching the internet and other media to find relevant and up to date information. * Analyse information gained from various sources to add to reports. * Assist in internal and external meetings as required. * Be able to provide explanation and reasons for decisions made. * Create and present reports as required. * Maintain company records and databases. * Identify and escalate any potential risks. * Assist with market and product development. * Other reasonable duties as instructed by the Senior Underwriter/Head of Department. **Skills and Experience Specification:** **Essential:** * Honours Bachelor’s degree, 2:1 or above, preferably in Law, Finance, Business or a related analytical discipline. * Excellent Communication skills * Professional and confident communicator who is able to build effective relationships with external facing stakeholders i.e., brokers/clients. * Good analytical skills * IT literate * Willingness to attend external and internal training courses. * Comfortable with traveling to different offices, which may include international travel. * Out of hours meetings i.e., networking/relationship building may be necessary. **What We Offer** The Tokio Marine HCC Group of Companies offers a competitive salary and employee benefit package. We are a successful, dynamic organization experiencing rapid growth and are seeking energetic and confident individuals to join our team of professionals. The Tokio Marine HCC Group of companies is an equal opportunity employer. Please visit www.tmhcc.com for more information about our companies. \#LI\-HJ1

title: Haemovigilance Officer
description: **Haemovigilance Officer Job Description** **Job Title:** Haemovigilance Officer **Hours:** Part Time 24 hrs per Week (Job Sharing ) **Location:** Pathology Laboratory **Reporting Arrangements** This post will work closely on a day\-to\-day basis with the Chief / Senior Medical Scientist \& Medical Scientists in Blood Transfusion and will report to the Consultant Haematologist and Laboratory Manager. The Haemovigilance Officer is professionally accountable to the Director of Nursing for matters relating to nursing practice. **Key Working Relationships:** The post holder will work closely with: The Laboratory Manager The Consultant Haematologist Senior Medical Scientist \& Medical Scientists working in Blood Transfusion The Laboratory Quality Manager The Director of Nursing for all matters relating to nursing practice **Purpose of the Role** The post holder will have responsibility to ensure that Beacon Hospital meets all national and international standards relating to safe transfusion practice. To maintain highest standards of transfusion safety within Beacon hospital based on best practice guidelines **Principal Duties and Responsibilities** The key responsibilities and duties of the Haemovigilance Officer in Beacon Hospital have been divided into five essential categories 1\. Clinical Knowledge 2\. Quality , Change management ,\& Audit 3\. Preparing , Delivering and Evaluating the Education programme 4\. Information Technology 5\. Personal Qualities **Clinical Knowledge** Must possess specially focused knowledge and skills in transfusion practice at a higher level than other clinical staff. Identifies and seeks to reduce wastage of blood / blood components Identifies and promotes appropriate transfusion alternatives Develops and manages systems for identifying , managing, reporting and closing off serious adverse events / reactions and near\-misses \& non\-conformances Provides leadership in clinical practice \& acts as a resource for clinical staff Generates and contributes to the development \& continual updating of clinical policies and procedures Seeks to advocate on behalf of patients and families to participate in decisions about their transfusion requirements **Quality , Risk \& Audit** To initiate , support and implement quality improvement initiatives To work closely with the laboratory quality officer to ensure legislative and regulatory requirements relating to Haemovigilance are met Ensure all Haemovigilance audits are completed as per annual laboratory audit schedule Uses audit results to identify areas for improvement Uses change management skills to develop and quality Haemovigilance service To carry out root cause analysis following adverse events / non\-conformances and recommend corrective/ preventative actions to improve transfusion safety To assist transfusion staff in post transfusion infection lookbacks and product recalls To investigate all reports of transfusion reactions / events and report to consultant haematologist / Hospital Transfusion Committee (HTC) To attend internal Quality meetings and collate monthly / quarterly key performance indictors (KPI) To ensure the hospital achieves 100% traceability for blood and Blood components **Preparing , Delivering \& Evaluating a Haemovigilance Education Programme** Develops \& delivers education programme for all staff involved in transfusion practice Evaluates effectiveness of training provided Establishes \& maintains systems for managing training records for all Haemovigilance training provided Identifies own continuing professional development needs and engages appropriately **Information Technology** Proficiency in IT skills relevant to the role Familiarity with electronic blood management systems (Blood Track or equivalent) **Personnel Qualities** Ability to work independently and as part of a wider multidisciplinary team Excellent communication / negotiating skills \& ability to liaise with individuals / groups at all grades both internally \& externally Has strong results focus and ability to achieve results through collaborative working Has a professional and dedicated attitude to the role Is an effective leader and positive driver for change Demonstrates commitment to further education relevant to the role where appropriate Is committed to building a professional network to remain up\-to\-date with current Haemovigilance issues Demonstrates a strong willingness and ability to operate in the flexible manner that is essential for the effective delivery of the role. **Essential / Desirable Criteria** **Clinical Nurse Manager Grade 2 or Senior Medical Scientist** **Essential Qualifications / Experience** Be a registered Nurse or CORU registered Medical Scientist Have a minimum of 5 yrs post registration experience Hold a post graduate qualification relevant to the role (Haemovigilance / Risk Management / Blood Transfusion / Haematology) Possess excellent organisational , written, interpersonal communication skills Proficient in IT skills (Word / Excel /PowerPoint ) **Desirable Qualifications / Experience** Previous Haemovigilance experience Experience in education / training Experience in root cause analysis / risk management / audit This job description is intended to be an outline of the areas of responsibility and deliverables at the time of its writing. As the hospital and the post holder develop this job description may be subject to review in light of the changing needs of the hospital'

title: ICT Security & Networks Administrator
description: **Necessary arrangements can be made at interview stage to accommodate the needs of candidates with disabilities. If you are having difficulty uploading your CV online or in fully accessing any information on this site please email** **Role Profile** **Job title:** ICT Security \& Networks Administrator **Division/Function:** Finance, Technology and Risk / IT **Reports to:** Head of ICT Security \& Networks **Job Family:** Technology **Career Zone:** Professional **Career Stream:** Support Professionals **Contract Type:** Permanent * Closing Date for Applications: 3rd July 2026\*\* **Summary Of The Business** The National Treasury Management Agency (NTMA) provides asset and liability management services to the Irish Government. It has evolved from a single function agency managing the National Debt to a manager of a complex portfolio of public assets and liabilities. Businesses managed by the NTMA include borrowing for the Exchequer and the management of the National Debt, the State Claims Agency, the New Economy and Recovery Authority (“NewERA”), the Ireland Strategic Investment Fund, National Development Finance Agency and the NTMA Future Ireland Funds business unit with responsibility for the management of the Future Ireland Fund and Infrastructure, Climate and Nature Fund. The NTMA also assigns staff to the National Asset Management Agency, the Strategic Banking Corporation of Ireland (SBCI) and Home Building Finance Ireland (HBFI). Working at the NTMA offers excellent benefits including: * Flexible work options including hybrid working and variable work hours * An extensive wellbeing programme * Excellent learning and development opportunities that allow for full career development within the organisation * A diverse range of initiatives: LGBT\+, Gender Matters, Disability Awareness, Sports \& Social, Volunteering and lots more * Reimbursement of annual professional membership fees * Career average Defined Benefit Pension Scheme **Role Summary** The role of the ICT Security \& Networks Administrator, reporting to the Head of ICT Cyber Security \& Networks, is to participate in delivering and maintaining the confidentiality, integrity and high availability of NTMA data and services to the business in a hybrid network model. The ICT Security \& Networks Administrator will assist in the configuration and management of networking and data communications infrastructure on premise and in a hybrid cloud environment. The role will also work with in a team assisting in the management of ICT Security technologies with a strong focus on network management, response to security system alerts and suspicious network activity. The role requires the individual maintains knowledge of relevant technical systems and professional certifications, keeping up to date on security and network risks, trends, and process innovations and their application to the security of the Agency’s business systems. **Principal Accountabilities** The ICT Security \& Networks Administrator will participate in managing and maintaining a secure network infrastructure and communication systems across the Agency with a strong emphasis on availability, security practices and monitoring tools. Duties will include, but not limited to, assisting with the following tasks: * Administration and support of network security control tools including firewalls, network access management and other network security elements * Assist in evaluating security of new products and services (on premise and cloud services) * Troubleshoot and analyse network security on various technologies * Monitor security system logs and reports for anomalies and problems * Support network security and system auditing and vulnerability assessments activities * Participate in remediation and mitigation of detected vulnerabilities **Required Qualifications** * Bachelor Degree in Computing, Engineering or equivalent qualification or experience * Certification/Qualifications in industry recognised Network and Security disciplines The following would be a distinct advantage: * Cisco CCNA/CCNP, Fortinet Certifications, Checkpoint CCSA, Palo Alto * Microsoft Network, Security, or Cloud certifications * Any relevant networking and IT security qualifications **Experience** * Minimum of 5 years’ experience working in a network security administration role for a medium size organisation * Solid knowledge and understanding of LAN, WAN, DMZ, VPN and DNS network technologies and protocols * Solid experience supporting network security management solutions * Solid knowledge and understanding of Public Key Infrastructure (PKI) * Working experience with security technologies such as Fortinet and Checkpoint * Working experience with web and email content filters, Intrusion Prevention Systems (IPS) * Solid understanding of cloud networking and security concepts * Experience with cloud\-based security solutions and best practice * Familiarity with security frameworks such as NIST, ISO 27001, and CIS * Experience working with ticketing tools for Incident, Change \& Problem Management Experience in the following areas would be a distinct advantage: * Experience supporting technologies include CISCO and Palo Alto * Experience with network security monitoring tools such as SIEM and NDR * Experience with Access and Identity Management and Privileged Assessment Management solutions * Experience with Microsoft Azure, including Azure Virtual Networks, Azure Firewall, and Azure Security Centre. **Skills** * Aptitude for solving problems and acting on own initiative. * Strong team player with a flexible approach. * Highly motivated with a willingness to adapt and learn new skills. * Effective communicator, both written and oral, with a positive and confident attitude. **Pension Abatement** If an appointee has previously been employed in the Civil or Public Service and that appointee is entitled to or in receipt of a pension from the Civil or Public Service or where a Civil/Public Service pension comes into payment during the appointee’s re\-employment that pension will be subject to abatement in accordance with Section 52 of the Public Service Pensions (Single Scheme and other Provisions) Act 2012\. Please note: In applying for this position, you are acknowledging that you understand that the abatement provisions, where relevant, will apply. It is not envisaged that the employing Department/Office/Body will support an application for an abatement waiver in respect of appointments to this position. Applicants will be required to complete a pre\-employment declaration to confirm whether they have previously availed of a Public sector pension scheme. **The NTMA is proud to be an equal opportunities employer. We recognise that a diverse and inclusive workplace informs better decision making, creative thinking, innovation and drives business performance. We are strongly committed to ensuring that our workforce is representative of our society.**

title: ICTS Project Engineer
description: We are currently seeking an ICTS Project Engineer who will play a critical role in delivering innovative security systems for our clients. This position offers the opportunity to work on diverse projects across Europe, collaborating with a talented team while advancing your expertise in the security industry. If you are passionate about technology and security and thrive in a dynamic environment, we invite you to explore this exciting opportunity with us. **Responsibilities** * This role of Project Engineer is primarily to work with specific customers to ensure the smooth running of projects. Additionally, there may be a requirement to continue in the role of Senior Installation Engineer, depending on workloads. * This role also requires extensive travel within Europe, with the need to stay away for periods of time. * The Project Engineer will be part of a team, which as a group, will perform the duties to install security systems at customer's premises * Supervise the work force, be the senior installation engineer and ensure full compliance with the design/specifications on any project. * On occasion the role will also involve Project Managing and installing any smaller orders that the company receives through either the Service or Installation Department when in country or on a site already **Requirements** * Proven experience in a similar role within the security industry * Strong knowledge of security systems including access control, CCTV, intrusion detection, and integrated security solutions * Strong problem\-solving abilities and analytical skills to address technical challenges effectively * Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills for building relationships with clients and team members * Willingness to travel to sites as needed and work flexible hours to meet project demands * The role requires a great deal of initiative, autonomy, and client liaison. **Benefits** * We are employee\-owned! Here at Evolution we have a unique employee ownership model which means that our entire team owns our business. You have the opportunity to become a beneficiary of our future success * 25 days annual leave \+ local bank holidays * Company pension scheme * 2 private health insurances * Optional annual private wellbeing and health screening appointment fully funded by Evolution * Employee assistance programme which offers 24/7 access to free health and wellbeing support * Life Assurance from day 1 (4x salary)

title: Installation Engineer
description: **Who We Are** Applied Materials is a global leader in materials engineering solutions used to produce virtually every new chip and advanced display in the world. We design, build and service cutting\-edge equipment that helps our customers manufacture display and semiconductor chips – the brains of devices we use every day. As the foundation of the global electronics industry, Applied enables the exciting technologies that literally connect our world – like AI and IoT. If you want to push the boundaries of materials science and engineering to create next generation technology, join us to deliver material innovation that changes the world. **What We Offer** Salary: €36,000\.00 \- €49,500\.00 Location: Leixlip,IRL You’ll benefit from a supportive work culture that encourages you to learn, develop, and grow your career as you take on challenges and drive innovative solutions for our customers. We empower our team to push the boundaries of what is possible—while learning every day in a supportive leading global company. Visit our Careers website to learn more. At Applied Materials, we care about the health and wellbeing of our employees. We’re committed to providing programs and support that encourage personal and professional growth and care for you at work, at home, or wherever you may go. Learn more about our benefits. As a Field Service Engineer \[Customer Engineer] at Applied Materials, you serve as the direct liaison with customers, collaborating closely to install, maintain, and upgrade equipment. You'll use digital analytics for troubleshooting and apply basic diagnostic techniques to assess and address technical issues. Your responsibilities include performing preventative and corrective maintenance on various systems such as electrical, vacuum, mechanical, plasma, hydraulic, and gas systems. Additionally, you'll coordinate and communicate directly with customers to ensure smooth operations and exceptional service. In order to support our long\-term development, we are looking for talents to join our teams in Europe (Dresden, Germany / Leixlip, Ireland / Grenoble, France). Within our Installation department, we are looking for our **Installation Engineer to support European regions** . The iTeam (Installation team) stands for the Installation of semiconductors production equipment across the European Region (mainly in Germany, ireland, France and Italy). We roll in, assemble, connect, and test the new systems until we hand over to our service team and our customers for the manufacturing of wafers to kick off. Being part of the Install Task Force is more than a job, it is a way of life. As a team **we travel approx. 75 % of our working time** , and also get opportunities to support installations worldwide. This position demands technical expertise, customer focus, team involvement and flexibility to travel. **Full training on our equipment will be provided through mentoring.** **Key Responsibilities** * Perform independently installation tasks (mechanical assembly, electrical connections, robot calibrations), from the integration to the inspection of the tools including troubleshooting in case of issues. * Follow all checklists and procedures and ensure correct procedures and schematics are available prior to performing work. * Test equipment with assistance from senior engineers, existing documentation and diagnostic techniques. * Respond to factory issues by communicating with other engineers and participating in escalation conference calls. * Participate in continuous improvement projects to reduce installation cycle time and costs. * Provide accurate reporting of status, hours worked and non\-conformances. * Adhere to the security policy and quality standards of Applied Materials and its customers. **Skills and Experience required:** * Bachelor or Master degree with technical background (mechanical assembly, equipment installation, maintenance, mechatronics, electronics, electrical, mechanical, or similar...) * Position open to junior profiles. * Troubleshooting skills to solve mechanical and electrical issues during system installation. * Ability to communicate daily pass\-downs and equipment status reports. * Project management skills to control installation project within budget, timeline and installation quality requirements. * Ability to work autonomously. * Good English communication skills both written and verbal. * Good Knowledge of the MS Office (Excel, Powerpoint) * The position requires extensive travelling throughout Europe (75% of the time, depending on overall activity requirements) **Physical Requirements:** This position often requires extensive walking, standing for extended periods of time, working in tight spaces, bending, squatting, twisting, kneeling, reaching, climbing stairs and ladders, working at height, lifting up to 35 lbs., the ability to distinguish between colors and working with electricity, the ability to work in an environment with noise that may be difficult for some individuals with sensitivity to noise, and the use of hand and power tools. Additionally, because this position generally involves working in a clean room, it requires the use of appropriate Personal Protective Equipment such as coveralls, hoods, booties, safety glasses, gloves, respirators, chemical aprons, and face shields. **Additional Information** **Time Type:** Full time **Employee Type:** Assignee / Regular **Travel:** Yes, 75% of the Time **Relocation Eligible:** No The salary offered to a selected candidate will be based on multiple factors including location, hire grade, job\-related knowledge, skills, experience, and with consideration of internal equity of our current team members. In addition to a comprehensive benefits package, candidates may be eligible for other forms of compensation such as participation in a bonus and a stock award program, as applicable. For all sales roles, the posted salary range is the Target Total Cash (TTC) range for the role, which is the sum of base salary and target bonus amount at 100% goal achievement. Applied Materials is an Equal Opportunity Employer. Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, national origin, citizenship, ancestry, religion, creed, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, disability, veteran or military status, or any other basis prohibited by law.

title: Intern, Student Operations
description: Sales \& Marketing Consultant (Part\-Time) \- Overture Tributary R0192050 Overture Tributary Birmingham, Alabama Full time Apply Now * Overview * Job Responsibilities * Success Profile * Trending * Benefits **Overview** Greystar is looking for dedicated and hard\-working individuals who want to help us continue to be the best at what we do. Today, we are the largest rental housing operator and developer in the US and one of the largest global investment management companies, delivering industry\-leading services to investors, clients, and residents. We offer unrivaled professional development and career growth opportunities to our team members and look forward to welcoming you to Greystar, where our people are what make us the Global Leader in Rental Housing. Back to navigation (Overview) Job Responsibilities **About Greystar** Greystar is a leading, fully integrated global real estate platform offering expertise in property management, investment management, development, and construction services in institutional\-quality rental housing. Headquartered in Charleston, South Carolina, Greystar manages and operates over $300 billion of real estate in more than 265 markets globally with offices throughout North America, Europe, South America, and the Asia\-Pacific region. Greystar is the largest operator of apartments in the United States, managing over one million units/beds globally. Across its platforms, Greystar has nearly $79 billion of assets under management, including over $35 billion of development assets and over $36\.5 billion of regulatory assets under management. Greystar was founded by Bob Faith in 1993 to become a provider of world\-class service in the rental residential real estate business. To learn more, visit www.greystar.com. **Job Description Summary** This part\-time role supports the growth and visibility of the Overture Tributary active adult community through strategic marketing and outreach efforts. Working 25–30 hours per week, the role partners with the community team and local community organizations to promote brand awareness, attract prospective residents aged 55\+, and drive qualified traffic to the community. This position plays a key role in supporting leasing performance, enhancing resident engagement, and contributing to overall occupancy and retention goals. **Job Description** * Develop and implement marketing strategies tailored to the 55\+ active adult demographic to drive community engagement and leasing success. * Assist with lead generation and research potential residents, referral sources, and local partnerships. * Collaborate with leasing and property management teams to ensure consistent and compelling messaging. * Represent the community at events, open houses, and local gatherings to generate excitement and interest. * Build and nurture relationships with prospective residents, families, senior organizations, and local influencers. * Leverage marketing channels such as social media, email, newsletters, and community events to reach target audiences. * Conduct market research to stay current on trends in active adult living and inform marketing efforts. * Assist in the design and distribution of marketing materials (flyers, brochures, presentations) aligned with brand standards. * Track and report on the success of marketing and outreach efforts with regular updates to leadership. * Attend community and industry events to increase brand visibility and referral partnerships. * Serve as a brand ambassador by reflecting the values and lifestyle of the community. * Provide on\-site support as needed for leasing activities, resident events, and retention programs. **Additional Compensation** Many factors go into determining employee pay within the posted range including business requirements, prior experience, current skills and geographical location. * Corporate Positions: In addition to the base salary, this role may be eligible to participate in a quarterly or annual bonus program based on individual and company performance. * Onsite Property Positions: In addition to the base salary, this role may be eligible to participate in weekly, monthly, and/or quarterly bonus programs. **Robust Benefits Offered\*:** * Competitive Medical, Dental, Vision, and Disability \& Life insurance benefits. title: Investment Analyst
description: **Job Title: Investment Analyst** **Sector: Global Investment Firm** **Location: Dublin \- Hybrid** **Salary: Base negotiable plus exceptional bonus opportunity and benefits** **Our Client** Our client are a Global Investment Firm. They have a track\-record of delivering exceptional results and have an excellent reputation globally. **Why should you apply?** This is an excellent opportunity for a driven and ambitious individual who is seeking to advance their career in an exciting industry. You will be part of an investment team that has exposure to all projects, large scale, niche, and highly specialised ventures. The role will potentially enable the successful candidate to put themselves on a path to a seat at the top table. **Who should apply?** Candidates with prior experience in finance with the following: * 2\-5 years’ experience in accounting, investment banking or structured finance role * 3rd level degree in accounting, economics, finance, actuary, or another numeric discipline * Excellent academics and proven track\-record of high achievement * Ability to analyse large data sets * Highly motivated to take on responsibilities * Post\-grad qualification in finance advantageous but not essential * Concise reporting writing ability * High level of proficiency in financial modelling * Strong attention to detail in both written and numerical data/documentation * Keen interest in financial markets **Role and Reporting Lines** Reporting directly to the Investment Manager works closely with other members of the Investment team, duties will include: * Financial modelling * Co\-ordinating due diligence and carrying out detailed market research * Creating and writing key reports for internal and external stakeholders * Assisting members of the wider team with ad\-hoc presentations and analysis * Reviewing equity assumptions * Stress testing and sensitivity analysis for all potential investments **Interested in this position?** To apply, please submit your CV to Aonghus Cody who is managing this assignment via the link below or to inquire further please contact Aonghus directly at 01 529 4200\.

title: Investment Counsellor
description: Our client is a leading independent investment firm with a global presence, managing assets for both institutional and private investors. With more than half a century of industry experience, they specialise in providing tailored investment strategies, guided by a research\-driven approach and a commitment to client success. Their dynamic and collaborative work environment fosters professional growth, innovation, and excellence in financial services. They are looking for an **Investment Counsellor** to join their expanding team in Dublin. **Job Profile for** **Investment Counsellor** Responsibilities will include but not be limited to: * Build long\-standing relationships with company clients, ensuring they always receive exceptional service * Manage a book of high\-net\-worth relationships with approximately €100\-150 million in assets * Be the conduit between company clients, the Investment Policy Committee, research and trading departments, and several service teams * Proactively update clients on portfolio changes, financial markets, and the company's current market strategy and investment philosophy * Gain advanced finance and capital markets education through the company training program * Maintain a positive personality and empathise with clients **Candidate Profile for Investment Counsellor** * Must be fluent in Dutch and English, both written and spoken * 2\+ years of exposure to private clients within the banking or wealth management industry * MiFID certification, training or the equivalent * CFA or similar education is beneficial * Knowledge of financial concepts, markets, current events and the market * Willing to connect with and educate a variety of audiences **What Our Client Offers** * 100% paid premiums for company top\-tier supplemental healthcare and dental plans for employees and their qualified dependents * 28 days annual leave, with the ability to purchase up to 3 additional days per year, plus up to 11 paid holidays * Enhanced maternity pay package with 26 weeks’ top\-up to full base pay for eligible employees * Fertility, hormonal health and family\-forming benefit * A retirement pension plan, featuring a 9% company pension contribution of your base pay and an additional company match of up to 5% of base pay * Monthly gym subsidy * Employee Assistance Program and other emotional well\-being services * A collaborative working environment that practices ongoing training, educational support and employee appreciation events * Relocation package

title: Junior Accountant
description: If you are starting out your career in the accounting field, and searching for a team with strong mentors, a clear progression pathway and structured development... then we should talk. Barden are delighted to be partnering with a successful Irish company in Kildare who are looking for a Junior Accountant to join their finance team. This person will initially look after the accounts receivable duties and develop from there. **ABOUT THE ROLE:** * Managing the end\-to\-end accounts receivable process. * Processing customer invoices, credit notes and account reconciliations. * Monitoring outstanding balances and carrying out regular debt collection activities. * Allocating payments and resolving customer queries. * Preparing regular aged debt reports and providing updates to key stakeholders. * Supporting month\-end activities, including reconciliations and reporting. * Liaising with internal teams and customers to resolve billing issues. **ABOUT THE PERSON:** * Previous experience in an accounts receivable or accounts assistant role. * Strong understanding of invoicing, reconciliations and cash collection processes. * Excellent attention to detail with strong organisational skills. * Confident communicator with strong stakeholder management skills. * A proactive, hands\-on approach with the ability to work independently and as part of a team.

title: Junior Company Secretary

collaborative and supportive team environment * Strong mentoring and career development opportunities * Exposure to a varied portfolio of clients and governance matters * Flexible working arrangements title: Junior HR Business Partner
description: Our client, a leading international organisation, is seeking an ambitious HR professional to join their team in a broad and commercially focused people role. This opportunity would suit an experienced HR Generalist looking to take the next step towards an HR Business Partner career path. **Key Responsibilities** * Partner with managers and stakeholders to support a range of people initiatives and day\-to\-day HR matters * Support talent attraction activities, workforce planning, and recruitment projects across the business * Contribute to employee engagement, learning and development, and early careers programmes * Analyse people data and workforce metrics, identifying trends and providing meaningful insights to support decision\-making * Support organisational change, talent development, and employee lifecycle activities * Build trusted relationships across the business and help drive a positive employee experience * Identify opportunities to improve HR processes, programmes, and ways of working **Skills \& Experience** * 4\+ years' HR experience within a Generalist, HR Advisor, or similar role * Experience gained within financial services, professional services, or another highly matrixed and commercially driven environment would be particularly beneficial. * Strong understanding of core HR practices across the employee lifecycle * Experience supporting managers and building effective stakeholder relationships * Analytical mindset with the ability to interpret and present people data * Excellent communication, organisational, and problem\-solving skills * Ambition to develop a career within an HR Business Partnering environment * Knowledge of Irish employment legislation is advantageous This is an excellent opportunity to join a high\-performing global organisation where you'll gain exposure to strategic people initiatives, partner with senior stakeholders, and further develop your HR Business Partnering capabilities within a collaborative and supportive environment.

title: Junior Sales Associate
description: Watch our Careers video on YouTube \- **https://youtu. be/9DzEDYP8MRc** **The Role.** For those interested in a career as a Financial Advisor, IPF is the place to be. We pay our people more, train them better, and invest more in their careers than any other financial institution or company in Ireland. We have a proud history of producing the top financial planners in the industry. You will be working towards becoming a Financial Advisor. As a key member of the team, you will be working with and learning from a team of established financial advisors. Working within our Career Path program, your initial duties \& responsibilities will consist of: * Reviewing/upselling existing customer benefit packages * Working to KPI’s and meeting sales targets * Setting up appointments for the Financial Advisors on your team * For full information, please see our Career Path \- https://www.ipf.ie/careers.html **The Company.** Irish Pensions \& Finance, formed in 1993, has over 30,000 clients and in excess of €1 billion in funds under management. Roughly 80% of our business book comes from the Public Sector, where we administer 152 Union\-sponsored, salary\-deducted AVC schemes. Though most of our 115 employees are based in our offices in Dublin 15, we cover the entire country with more and more of our business being transacted remotely. **The Successful Candidate.** We are looking for individuals who are ready to commit to a career as a Financial Advisor. This is a consultative sales\-focused position. You must have one year of sales experience and have a track record in consistently hitting and exceeding KPIs. The candidate must be comfortable making calls, listening to clients, building solutions, and working to KPI’s \& targets. The approach we take with our clients is very much consultative, and our objective is to have advisors with necessary qualifications, integrity, and commitment to give clients the correct advice. You must be focused, ambitious and have a realistic understanding of the relationship between hard work and success. We can only work with people whose integrity is beyond reproach. **The Package.** * Year 1: €55k OTE (Basic, Bonus, Commission) * Year 2: €75k OTE (Basic, Bonus, Commission plus expenses) * Year 3: €90k OTE (Basic, Bonus, Commission plus expenses) * Year 4 \+ (Senior Financial Planner) €120k. A sign\-on bonus is applicable to candidates with an APA or QFA designation. Inclusion in Company Pension Scheme. Inclusion in Company Life Cover Scheme after year 1\. Extra annual leave day every year for the first five years. Wellness days after 6 months. **Education.** An Education bursary of up to €6,000 is available. This is paid towards qualifying the employee as a Financial Planner (QFA \& CFP exams). \*Please note this role is hybrid\-based, time is split between office \& home. Full\-time attendance in the office is required for the first 4\-5 weeks for training \& induction purposes. \*\*Only applications that are accompanied with a Cover Letter, outlining why you want this specific role, will be considered. *Due to the very high number of applications received for this role, we will only be in contact with candidates whom we are inviting to the first interview stage. Thank you for your understanding.* Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €32,000\.00\-€70,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Additional leave * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Company pension * On\-site parking * Wellness program * Work from home Ability to commute/relocate: * DUBLIN 15, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (preferred) Application question(s): * Do you have a QFA or APA designation? Experience: * sales: 1 year (preferred) Work Location: Hybrid remote in DUBLIN 15, County Dublin

title: Junior Sous Chef (M/F/X)
description: Big Mamma is a French Italian restaurant group, the brainchild of Victor Lugger and Tigrane Seydoux. Over the past 10 years we have opened the doors of 36 restaurants across Europe (France, Monaco, the United Kingdom, Spain, Germany, Italy, Belgium and Ireland), the United Arab Emirates (Dubai) and Mexico (Mexico City). Our mission? To share the special ambience of the most beautiful places of Italian tradition, creating unique moments of sharing with our customers. **Take the lead with La Squadra as JUNIOR SOUS CHEF in Dublin** With Big Mamma’s rapid expansion, we are looking for a talented **Junior Sous Chef** to join us at *Gloria Osteria (41 Westmoreland St, Dublin)* and become the *leader* and *Chef* of tomorrow. **Your main tasks:** **FROM CREATION TO PLATE EXECUTION** * Participate in the menu change process alongside the Executive Chefs, contributing to a unique culinary experience. * Speed and precision at the pass will be your hallmark. * Ensure every dish is perfect, adhering to the Group's recipes and standards. **OPERATIONAL EFFICIENCY** * You are the Chef’s right hand, ensuring everything is impeccable: compliance with hygiene standards, a well\-organized team, and checks always aligned with objectives. * Manage Food Costs like a true leader: zero waste, flawless inventories, and always on\-time orders. * Plan, organize, and lead the brigade with energy and precision. **SUCCESS IS BUILT ON TEAMWORK** * Train and support your team daily, sharing passion and professionalism. * Build your Squad: your leadership creates harmony and makes the workplace stimulating and positive. **Our offer:** * Highly competitive salary. * Permanent contract with 2 days off consequently. * A meal prepared daily with love by our Squadra and a discount in all our trattos. * Numerous training and career opportunities. * International mobility across all our venues. * *AND much more!* **BIG MAMMA is an equal opportunity employer.** Qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, race, color, religion, national origin, disability, age, or any other characteristic protected by law. \#JoinBigMamma

title: L1 Support Engineer
description: * Dublin City Centre Location * Hybrid Model \- 2 Days/Week WFH (After an initial 6 weeks period) * €35k\-€40k/year DOE \+ On\-call \+ Benefits **Your Responsabilities** * Manage all Incidents and service requests within SLA * Log Incidents and Requests accurately in the PSA Tool (Autotask) * Resolve technical problems with software applications or network systems * Record all actions, updates and time in a clear and concise way in the PSA Tool (Autotask) * Ensure call handling/quality, ticket management/quality, resolution of issues meet the required standards * Escalate incidents to higher level when unable to resolve * Create new/update existing Knowledge Articles within documentation platform (ITGlue) * Assist with Problem Management activities, supporting a drive to reduce unnecessary/avoidable contacts to the Service Desk * Always consider the experience of the end user and strive to make every contact with the Service Desk a positive experience in as far possible * Must be a self\-starter capable of working on one’s own initiative **Your Experience And Education** * 2\+ years in a similar role * MSP background is a strong advantage * Excellent Trouble Shooting skills and must be able to demonstrate by way of example * Fluent English and excellent written, verbal communication skills with attention to detail * Able to think through, communicate and present information effectively to all levels within the organisation * A flexible, can\-do attitude * IT qualification Diploma/Degree * Microsoft Certifications desirable * Active Directory Administration * Office 365 Administration * Excellent Desktop skills **Please APPLY directly or contact me at sarao@therecruitmentcompany.com for more info.**

title: Laboratory Solution Specialist
description: **The Laboratory Solution Specialist** is responsible for providing technical expertise, solutions and consultation to prospective customers, as part of the sales process with a deep understanding of laboratory workflows and strong communication skills to demonstrate the value of our diagnostic products and solutions. This position reports to the Head of Solution \& Design UK \& Ireland and is part of the Sales Team working remotely to cover Ireland and UK region. **In this role, you will have the opportunity to:** * **Customer Collaboration** : Engage with customers to understand their unique laboratory requirements and challenges. Build strong relationships to gather insights into their needs, using a value\-selling approach to develop these needs into explicit demands. * **Solution Design** : Based on the insights gained from customer interactions, the specialist is responsible for designing laboratory solutions that address these needs. This may involve recommending specific analyzers, processes, and assays that align with the customer's goals and requirements. Adapt solutions to include 3rd party innovations as required. Deliver the solution benefits to a spectrum of customer in one to one and/or group presentations. Having the ability to articulate the Company solution benefits in line with the customer needs for today and tomorrow. * Collaborate with customers to highlight the urgency in addressing their explicit needs. Differentiate our Automation and Workflow IT Solutions using USPs using metrics that articulate the benefits clearly. * **Collaboration with Sales Team** : The specialist collaborates closely with the Ireland and UK sales team to ensure that the proposed laboratory solutions align with the company's sales strategy. This involves providing sales professionals with the necessary product knowledge and support to effectively promote and sell these solutions to customers. * **Product Knowledge** : Staying up\-to\-date with the latest developments in laboratory automation, chemistry, and immunoassay technologies is essential. The specialist must possess a deep understanding of Company’s product offerings and industry trends. The specialist may also be responsible for training customers and the sales team on the proper use of the laboratory solutions and providing ongoing support. **The essential requirements of the job include:** * A minimum of 3 years clinical laboratory experience either in an HSE Pathology laboratory or commercial environment * BMS Qualification or Biomedical Science (or related) undergraduate degree * Exceptionally demonstrated planning and organization skills * Familiarity with business management systems including CAD software. * Automation experience within HSE laboratories **Travel, Motor Vehicle Record \& Physical/Environment Requirements:** * Ability to travel – Primarily within Ireland, with occasional assignments to other regions based on business needs. Up to 70% travel, including overnight stays, will be required within the designated territory or other specified locations. * Must have a valid driver’s license with an acceptable driving record **It would be a plus if you also possess previous experience in:** * Automation solution design experience for NHS/HSE laboratories * Technical tender response submission experience * Commercial experience is a plus but not essential

title: Manufacturing Engineer
description: Our client is a leading global medical device manufacturer based in Dublin, dedicated to the design and manufacture of injectable drug delivery and containment systems. They operate to the highest quality and regulatory standards within an FDA\-regulated, ISO 13485 environment. They are currently seeking a **Process Engineer** to join their Dublin site on an initial 6\-month contract, supporting a critical preventive maintenance, calibration and FDA audit\-readiness initiative. This is an on\-site role with an immediate start. **Responsibilities** * Develop and document preventive maintenance procedures and processes for manufacturing equipment and utilities * Develop and standardise calibration procedures for critical process and utility instrumentation * Review existing maintenance and calibration documentation, identify gaps, and define what is required to bring the site to FDA audit readiness * Build the supporting procedures, justifications and documentation required for inspection readiness * Work cross\-functionally with Engineering, Maintenance, Quality and Manufacturing teams to embed compliant, audit\-ready processes * Support continuous improvement of maintenance reliability and compliance programmes **Experience** * Engineering background (Mechanical, Manufacturing, Process or related discipline) with experience that has moved into process development around preventive maintenance and calibration * Minimum 4 years' experience in an FDA\-regulated manufacturing environment (medical device, pharmaceutical or biotechnology) * Demonstrated experience developing PM and calibration procedures and documentation * Experience supporting FDA audit readiness, including reviewing current practices, identifying gaps and preparing justifications * Strong technical writing and documentation skills, with the ability to build and structure compliant processes * Knowledge of GMP and FDA / ISO 13485 requirements; CMMS experience (e.g. SAP PM) an advantage **To learn more about this role apply online or contact Lewis Murray @lewis.murray@hrmrecruit.com for a confidential discussion.**

title: Mobile Service Technician
description: Ponsse is one of the world’s largest manufacturers of cut\-to\-length forest machines. We are focusing on succeeding with our customers and oriented to people. We want to be the preferred partner for responsible forestry and to reach that we combine great people working together with high tech machinery and cutting\-edge digital technologies. **Workplace** Based at Head Office at Portlaoise, with travel to various sites **Line Manager** Service Manager **Working Hours** 08:00 to 17:00, Monday to Friday. Or Extended hours if required to complete the role. **Main Duties** * To work in a safe and effective manner protecting themselves and their colleagues and customers * Keep a safe and tidy workplace * To identify and repair machinery as directed by their line manager * Be prepared to be flexible in hours worked as the role requires * To complete task\-based risk assessments and safe systems of work to ensure that hazards are identified, and the work is safely carried out * To help colleagues with their tasks when required following the safe system of work * Identify the machine they are working on by job number or serial number * Risk Assessments/Timesheets/Job Cards are part of your working day, these documents are to be completed daily * Any jobs completed on site or in the workshop are to be approved by the service manager * Due to the nature of the work, you must be prepared to stay overnight where necessary and carry a change of clothes, this also enables you to put on dry clothing. * As much of your work is on site you must be available to travel and stay away from the head office and Home at short notice * Only use equipment on which they have been trained and authorised to use * Carry out daily checks on equipment before they use it * Follow company and site rules, procedures, and policies * Ensure a high standard of housekeeping both in the workshop/site, and yard due to being seen by customers and visitors * Wear the appropriate PPE when required and take care of all issued PPE * Report any incidents or near misses * The company vehicle is for business use only * Keep the company vehicle in a tidy condition, serviced in line with manufacturers requirements and keep vehicle in a fully roadworthy condition. * Ensure regular safety checks are carried out to confirm the vehicle is safe and legal. * Ensure that rest breaks are taken and that working hours are exceedingly long. Driving is classed as working * Attend all internal H\&S training. **Support Duties** * Greet visitors and external customer in a polite and helpful manner * Wear the corporate clothing as issued * Follow the One Ponsse principles * ake an active role in completing Risk Assessments and Safe Systems of Work * Undertake required training to enable the role to be effectively carried out * Cooperate with the management during any investigation **For More Information, Please Contact** Terry Keogh, Service Manager terry.keogh@ponsse.com \+353858568232 *Ponsse wants to develop a working culture and environment that helps to bring the best out of everyone as individuals and us all together.* *Customer is always in the center of our work and we continuously encourage open and proactive communication, teamwork and taking responsibility as well as agile execution.* *We truly care about our people, their wellbeing and growth. We are Ponsse family.*

title: National Coach Developer
description: **Job Title:** National Coach Developer **Location:** FAI HQ, Abbottstown or Regional **Direct Reporting Line:** Coach Development Manager **Duration:** Permanent / Full\-Time **Work Model:** Hybrid **Introduction** The Football Association of Ireland (FAI) is the national governing body for football in Ireland, driving the growth, promotion, and development of the game at all levels. We are passionate about inspiring participation, supporting talent, and celebrating the power of football to unite communities across the country. With a proud history and an ambitious vision for the future, the FAI is committed to fostering excellence on and off the pitch, ensuring the game is inclusive, accessible, and a source of national pride. **Purpose of the Role** The **National Coach Developer** will lead the delivery and development of the FAI’s Coach Development programmes, supporting coaches from entry\-level to UEFA licence pathways. The role will create and deliver high\-quality formal and informal learning opportunities, enhance coach skills and knowledge, and contribute to the professional growth of coaches across Ireland. **Key Responsibilities** Coach Development Pathways * Deliver formal coaching courses crossing the full Coach Development Pathway, from entry\-level to UEFA qualifications. * Facilitate engaging, inclusive, and high\-quality learning environments in both classroom and on\-pitch settings. * Provide ongoing mentoring, observation, and feedback to support coaches’ development beyond formal courses. * Develop and maintain informal learning initiatives, including communities of practice, workshops, peer learning, and online forums. * Contribute to the design and enhancement of coach development materials, resources, and assessment processes. Collaboration \& Stakeholder Engagement * Represent the FAI at regional and national events, promoting coaching excellence and development programmes. * Collaborate with Regional FAI staff, tutors, mentors, and player development programmes to ensure alignment across pathways. * Build partnerships with educational institutions. **Person Specification** **Experience and Qualifications** Essential: * UEFA B Licence. * Track record in designing, delivering, and evaluating coach development programmes. * Significant coaching and/or coach development experience. * Experience mentoring or supporting coaches in formal and informal settings. * Excellent communication, facilitation, and relationship\-management skills. * Strong administrative skills and familiarity with relevant IT systems. * Experience managing projects and working collaboratively. * Experience delivering learner\-centred, reality\-based coaching environments. * Strong understanding of the UEFA Coaching Convention and FAI Coach Education framework. * Full Clean Driving licence. title: Network Engineer, Deployment & Support
description: Meta is looking for a Network Deployment \& Support Engineer who will be responsible for the deployment and support of one of the world’s largest and most complex networks. As a member of this team, you will have a unique opportunity to be involved in projects related to implementation \& scaling of Meta’s network. You will need to work with a constant sense of urgency continually growing a constantly evolving network. This team is responsible for planning and scaling one of the world's largest backbone and edge networks. As a Network Deployment Engineer, you will propose new ideas, gather constructive feedback from others, and push forward to make these ideas a reality. Network Engineer, Deployment \& Support Responsibilities: * Deploy and support our global production including edge, backbone, data center, campus, and content delivery network (CDN) infrastructure * Work cross\-functionally with project management, network engineering to generate implementation plans including Method of Procedure (MOP) and Bill of Materials (BOM), Engineering Design Packages (EDP), Scope of Works (SOW), Network equipment and server Rack Face Elevations (RFE) * Troubleshoot and resolve complex network issues, collaborate as needed to ensure timely resolution * Provide technical leadership and guidance during network deployment and migration activities in our data centers and POPs, including in the areas of facility power, cooling, rack layout and cable management, physical equipment installation, network equipment configuration, testing and handoff for production traffic * Drive problem management initiatives to solve complex issues that span multiple organizations. Initiatives exist globally across technical infrastructure reliability, policy, security, compliance, and business strategy * Help drive and align on workflow management tool requirements \- efficiency and automation roadmaps * Manage key vendor relationships including contract negotiations, pricing, and evaluation of current and potential partnerships * Manage partner engineering team relationships to enable the team(s) to effectively meet business needs * Develop and maintain technical documentation for network infrastructure and services * Provide input to the scaling and evolution of the team to meet the projected network demands * Up to 10% to 30% of travel Minimum Qualifications: * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, relevant technical field, or equivalent practical experience * 5\+ years of IP networking experience with deploying, migrating and supporting service providers, data center, and/or enterprise network infrastructure * Experience configuring and troubleshooting IP routing/switching technologies and protocols including TCP/IP, BGP, ISIS, MPLS, RSVP and SDN * Experience coordinating and executing hardware installation and configuration * Hands\-on experience with enterprise and carrier class routing, switching, and optical transport platforms * Knowledge of data center design and operational best practices * Experience with Linux OS/networking * Experience analyzing situations and utilizing problem solving experiences under pressure * Proven experience executing network deployment and/or migration projects * Experience interacting with vendors and external parties to drive projects to completion Preferred Qualifications: * Experience working in a multi\-vendor network environment * Experience with field\-based work in Collocation Facilities, Internet Exchanges, and Central Offices * Scripting experience with Python or Perl * Network certifications such as CCIE, CCNP or JNCIP * Experience with process improvement and systems development, leveraging automation to streamline repetitive workflows * Demonstrated ability to integrate AI tools to optimize/redesign workflows and drive measurable impact (e.g., efficiency gains, quality improvements) * Experience adhering to and implementing responsible, ethical AI practices (e.g., risk assessment, bias mitigation, quality and accuracy reviews) * Demonstrated ongoing AI skill development (e.g., prompt/context engineering, agent orchestration) and staying current with emerging AI technologies About Meta: Meta builds technologies that help people connect, find communities, and grow businesses. When Facebook launched in 2004, it changed the way people connect. Apps like Messenger, Instagram and WhatsApp further empowered billions around the world. Now, Meta is moving beyond 2D screens toward immersive experiences like augmented and virtual reality to help build the next evolution in social technology. People who choose to build their careers by building with us at Meta help shape a future that will take us beyond what digital connection makes possible today—beyond the constraints of screens, the limits of distance, and even the rules of physics. Individual compensation is determined by skills, qualifications, experience, and location. Compensation details listed in this posting reflect the base hourly rate, monthly rate, or annual salary only, and do not include bonus, equity or sales incentives, if applicable. In addition to base compensation, Meta offers benefits. Learn more about benefits at Meta.

title: OEM Business & Market Development – Drive Products
description: At **ABB** , we help industries run leaner and cleaner—and every person here makes that happen. You’ll be empowered to lead, supported to grow, and proud of the impact we create together. Join us and help run what runs the world. **This position reports to:** Local Division Manager \_\_ In this role you will be responsible for driving the sales strategy and G2M model for sales in the Drive Products and IEC LV Induction motors market within Irelands OEM (Original Equipment Manufacturers), machine building and industrial market sector, aligned with the division strategy. This includes low voltage drives, PLC \& HMI, and IEC low voltage motors offering to different types of OEMs and machine builders across different segments and applications within the Irish and Northern Irish market. Delivers both qualitative and quantitative sales targets to ensure sustainable order growth, revenues, profitability, market share, and customer satisfaction. Responsible to identify and developing new OEM sales opportunities. **Our Team Dynamics** Our teams support each other, collaborate, and never stop learning. Everyone brings something unique, and together we push ideas forward to solve real problems. Being part of our team means your work matters \- because the progress we make here creates real impact out there. **What’s in it for you?** We want you to bring your full self to work—your ideas, your energy, your ambition. You’ll have the tools and freedom to grow your skills, shape your path, and take on challenges that matter. Here, your work creates impact you can see and feel, every day. **Your Role And Responsibilities** In this role, you’ll help run what runs the world, by taking on meaningful work that drives real impact. * Work model: Office based with travel and flexibility. * Review the local market, identify and execute predefined market strategies. * Drive volume, mix, and profitability targets for selected customers across the division. * Build and maintain strategic customer relationships to understand their needs and position ABB's drive product solutions. * Leverage cross\-division synergies to enhance customer value and ensure a seamless sales experience. * Develop short\-to\-medium term sales plans using ABB tools. * Conduct regular pipeline reviews and propose recovery actions for any shortfalls. * Prepare competitive offers in coordination with the bid and proposal team, ensuring technical and financial accuracy. * Validate pricing, trading conditions, and margins on all proposals. * Communicate delivery timelines, payment terms, and contractual conditions aligned with ABB strategy. * Develop and communicate value propositions through marketing activities. * Manage sales administration procedures, commercial decisions, and support project management as needed. **Qualifications For The Role** * Degree in Automation, Electrical Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, or related field * Marketing / Business qualification or some time working in a similar business environment with a track record of building customer relationships considered a bonus * Previous experience in engaging business to position ABB as a valued solutions provider vrs competitors * Results\-oriented mindset combined with the engineering and business acumen * Strategic in approach and confidence to take initiative, and turn ideas into action * Strong communication and collaboration skills with the ability to build trust across stakeholders * Genuine drive to learn, grow, and continuously improve, both personally and as a sales individual **Benefits** ABB offers a full range of benefits to help you thrive at work and beyond. * Competitive Salary * Private Medical Allowance for Yourself and Family * Competitive Contributory Pension Scheme * Employee Share Acquisition Plan * Internal Mobility Opportunities, Mentorships \& Access to Projects Globally * Employee Assistance Programme * Generous Employee Referral Scheme * 25 Days Annual Leave Plus Bank Holidays * Company Car * Flexible Working * Generous Bonus Scheme **More About Us** ABB's Drive Products and IEC Low Voltage Motors division delivers motors, integrated motors, drives, and automation solutions for OEMs and machine builders globally. Our comprehensive portfolio combines ultra premium\-efficiency IEC Low Voltage Motors, variable frequency drives, AC500 PLCs, and HMIs to create optimised automation packages that reduce costs, reduce downtime, improve process throughput, and lower operational carbon footprint. With electric motors consuming 45% of global electricity and high\-efficiency systems cutting consumption by 10%, our integrated solutions help OEMs across blue\-chip manufacturing, water, food \& beverage, mining, and data centers build smarter, more efficient machines and operations. Join us. Be part of the team where progress happens, industries transform, and your work shapes the world. Run What Runs the World. \#ABBCareers Building a cleaner, smarter future takes all kinds of minds: the curious, the courageous, and the creative. That's why we welcome people from all backgrounds and experiences. Ready to make an impact? Apply today or visit **https://www.abb.com** to learn more about the impact of our solutions across the globe.

description: IT Contracting has gotten a boost with a great Platform Engineering opportunity with one of our clients in their Dublin offices! **About Your New Employer** * You will be working with one of the world's leading financial institutions, serving individual consumers, small and middle\-market businesses, and large corporations with a full range of banking, investing, asset management, and other financial and risk management products and services. * They are known for their commitment to innovation and technology, providing cutting\-edge digital banking solutions and services to enhance customer experience and streamline financial operations. * They are dedicated to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace, promoting a culture where employees from all backgrounds can thrive and contribute to the company's success. **About Your New Job as a Platform Engineer** * You will work as a key contributor within the cloud platform team, shaping and supporting robust cloud\-native environments in AWS. * Actively design, automate, and maintain secure, production\-grade infrastructure leveraging Infrastructure as Code (Terraform) and CI/CD pipelines. * Collaborate with security, engineering, and operations teams to deliver well\-architected cloud platforms aligned with industry and regulatory best practices. * Lead on cloud security, identity management, and networking, including implementation of zero\-trust architecture, governance, data encryption, and multi\-account setups. * Support cost and performance optimisation across diverse, scalable cloud environments and drive innovation through the adoption of automated workflows. . **What Skills You Need in this job as a Platform Engineer** * Senior\-level experience in software/platform engineering or site reliability engineering, including direct AWS experience. * Proven expertise in building and operating cloud environments in regulated industries;
